title: Accessibility to Improved Water Point Sources — 2SFCA
subtitle: Spatial accessibility to improved water sources in Sibi, Ghana.
description: Models household accessibility to boreholes and standpipes using the Two-Step Floating Catchment Area method with 500m walking catchments — Ghana's national standard for improved access.
Key findings: Peripheral households face limited access due to sparse facility coverage.
Low-access clusters highlight priority areas for new water points.

title: Accessibility to Dentist — 2SFCA
subtitle: Spatial accessibility to dental care in Washington DC.
description: Computes dental care accessibility across Washington DC using 10-mile driving catchments and the Two-Step Floating Catchment Area method.
Key findings: Higher accessibility clusters around the city centre where facility density is greatest.
Peripheral and southeastern areas show significant access gaps, indicating need for strategic facility siting.

title: NDVI & Land Surface Temperature
subtitle: Vegetation–temperature dynamics for urban heat analysis.
description: MODIS NDVI and LST time series analysed to reveal inverse relationships between greenness and surface temperature. Hotspots indicate where greening could reduce heat exposure.

title: Rainwater Harvesting Suitability
subtitle: Multi-criteria suitability analysis for rainwater harvesting.
description: Weighted overlay of slope, roof area, rainfall, and building proximity to identify optimal rainwater harvesting sites and enhance community water security.

title: Flood Risk Mapping
subtitle: Hydrologic flood susceptibility modelling.
description: Flow direction, accumulation, slope, land cover and soil data combined into a flood susceptibility index to support mitigation planning and infrastructure prioritisation.

title: LiDAR Canopy Height Model
subtitle: DSM, DTM and CHM for urban greenness assessment.
description: LiDAR point clouds processed into terrain and surface models. The canopy height model quantifies vegetation structure and identifies low-shade urban areas.
